records.ety: [Gr. <?/ to see through; <?/ = dia` through + <?/ to see; cf. <?/ that which is seen, a sight: cf. F. diorama. Cf. Panorama.]

defs: A mode of scenic representation, invented by Daguerre and Bouton, in which a painting is seen from a distance through a large opening. By a combination of transparent and opaque painting, and of transmitted and reflected light, and by contrivances such as screens and shutters, much diversity of scenic effect is produced.
A building used for such an exhibition.
